subtitle: A beach made of the eroded core of an Eocene granite pluton, where you wade and scramble through boulders 40 feet across to reach Devil's Bay.
spike_statement: The boulders here are the exhumed innards of a granite pluton that crystallised kilometres underground about 45 million years ago.

levers.photo_tone: warm granite, hard tropical noon light, turquoise water in shadowed grottoes
